quick_facts.label: Award and naming
quick_facts.value: This undergraduate allied-health pathway is offered under closely related titles, including B.Sc. in Emergency Medical Technology, B.Sc. Emergency and Trauma Care Technology, and Bachelor of Emergency Medical Technologist (Paramedic); applicants should verify the exact award title and curriculum at the admitting institution.

quick_facts.label: Typical duration
quick_facts.value: Published institutional structures commonly specify four years, generally described as three academic years plus a one-year internship, though the exact design should be checked in the university’s current regulations.

quick_facts.label: Typical entry qualification
quick_facts.value: A common requirement is Class 12 / 10+2 science with Physics, Chemistry and Biology; institutions may additionally set a minimum percentage, English requirement, age threshold, medical-fitness requirement, or category-specific relaxation.

quick_facts.label: Training focus
quick_facts.value: Published course descriptions combine emergency and trauma care with pre-hospital/ambulance practice, clinical education, life-support and airway skills, emergency equipment and procedures, and supervised internship or externship.

sections.heading: Eligibility: science at Class 12 is the usual route
paragraphs: There is no single admission rule demonstrated by the sources reviewed; each university publishes its own conditions. ICFAI University Tripura lists 10+2 science with PCB and 45% marks, with a stated relaxation for SC/ST/OBC candidates. Apollo University requires 10+2/equivalent science subjects and specifies 45% marks (40% for reserved-category candidates) plus age 17 by 31 December of the calendar year. Dr. D. Y. Patil Vidyapeeth specifies Physics, Chemistry and Biology/Botany and Zoology, age 17 by admission or by 31 December, and a government-medical-officer-endorsed fitness certificate.

Applicants should treat the programme brochure and the university’s current admissions notice—not a generic course listing—as controlling. Required subject combinations, percentage thresholds, age cut-offs, equivalency treatment, category rules, and fitness documentation can differ by institution and admission cycle.
bullets: Prepare Class 10 and Class 12 certificates/marksheets, identity and category/domicile documents where applicable, and any medical-fitness documentation requested by the university.
If applying with a diploma or for lateral entry, confirm that the institution recognises the qualification and specifies the required prior instructional hours.

sections.heading: Duration and clinical training
paragraphs: A four-year structure is the clearest recurring model in the reviewed institutional sources. Dr. D. Y. Patil Vidyapeeth describes its undergraduate B.Sc. structure as three years plus one year of internship; ICFAI University Tripura describes a four-year full-time programme with internship/externship and project in the fourth year; Apollo University describes its related Emergency and Trauma Care Technology programme as four years.

Because the academic and internship calendars are institution-specific, candidates should check whether the internship is credit-bearing, whether it is arranged by the college, the clinical-site requirements, attendance rules, insurance/health requirements, and whether any additional fees apply.
bullets: Common published format: 3 academic years + 1 internship year.
A lateral-entry route may be available at some institutions, but it is not universal and is subject to diploma and curriculum-hour equivalence.

sections.heading: Curriculum: emergency-care science, technical procedures, and clinical education
paragraphs: The curriculum is clinically oriented rather than a general science degree. ICFAI University Tripura’s published structure starts with anatomy and physiology, hospital and health systems, medical terminology/records, computer applications, and introduction to emergency medical services. It then includes microbiology, pathology, biochemistry, pharmacology, medical law and ethics, research methods and biostatistics, airway management, emergency instruments and procedures, and progressively directed clinical education.

The same published structure culminates in medical-emergency modules, directed clinical education, and internship/externship plus project. Its programme description also identifies skills such as IV cannulation, oxygen therapy, physical examination, AED use, airway maintenance, CPR, spinal immobilisation, bleeding control and fracture management. Actual scope of practice in placements and employment remains subject to clinical supervision, employer protocols, and applicable law.
bullets: Foundational biomedical sciences: anatomy, physiology, microbiology, pathology, biochemistry and pharmacology.
Emergency-practice content: EMS systems, emergency procedures and instrumentation, airway management, medical emergencies, patient safety, law and ethics.
Applied learning: clinical education/rotations followed by internship or externship; simulation-based learning is also highlighted by Apollo University.

sections.heading: Admission: process varies by university
paragraphs: Admission methods are not standardised across the reviewed providers. ICFAI University Tripura states that selection is based on merit through an application and interview, with certificate verification at interview. In contrast, Dr. D. Y. Patil Vidyapeeth states that applicants for its undergraduate B.Sc. programme must appear for its entrance examination. Students should therefore confirm the exact selection route before applying rather than assuming that a national entrance examination, merit-only selection, or an interview will apply everywhere.

Timelines are especially variable. A course page can retain a prior-year calendar, so applicants should obtain the current-cycle prospectus or admission notice directly from the university before paying an application or seat-confirmation fee.
bullets: Check whether selection is merit-based, an institutional/state entrance test, interview-based, or a combination.
Verify document-verification steps, provisional-admission conditions for awaiting results, reservation/domicile rules, and refund terms before payment.

sections.heading: Costs: published examples are variable and date-sensitive
paragraphs: Fees differ substantially by institution and may exclude accommodation, transport, mess, uniforms, clinical requirements, examination charges, insurance, or refundable deposits. As a published example, ICFAI University Tripura lists a total programme fee of ₹2,30,000 for its B.Sc. Emergency Medical Technology programme, plus a ₹25,000 admission fee, ₹10,000 refundable caution deposit and separately listed activity/alumni/hostel charges. Apollo University lists a total course fee of ₹5,39,000 for its four-year B.Sc. Emergency Medical Technology programme, including the charges shown on its course page.

These figures are institutional website examples available at review, not a national fee benchmark or a quote for a particular applicant. Fees, scholarship criteria, instalments, deposits, and hostel charges can change by academic year; obtain a written fee schedule for the relevant intake.
bullets: Compare the total payable amount, not just annual tuition.
Ask whether the internship year has tuition/recurring fees and whether clinical consumables, certifications, transport, hostel and mess costs are additional.
Confirm scholarship eligibility and whether an offer is renewable each year.

sections.heading: After graduation: emergency, pre-hospital, and critical-care settings
paragraphs: The degree is designed for technical allied-health work around acute illness and injury. Institutional descriptions identify emergency medical services, ambulance/pre-hospital care, hospital emergency departments, ICUs/CCUs, nursing homes, community health centres, medical colleges and specialty hospitals as relevant practice settings. The precise job title, permitted duties, required registration/certification, and supervision arrangements depend on the employer, state rules, clinical protocols, and the graduate’s competencies.

A further study route may be possible through institution-specific postgraduate allied-health, public-health, healthcare-management, education, or research programmes, but eligibility is programme-specific. Graduates should check the receiving institution’s published entry rules rather than assume that every postgraduate degree accepts this qualification.
bullets: Assess vacancies by required competencies and clinical exposure, not title alone: some employers may use titles such as emergency medical technologist, emergency/trauma-care technician, or paramedic.
Maintain evidence of internship/clinical rotations, life-support training where applicable, and academic transcripts for employer or further-study applications.
